notes:
My widescreen (3440x1440) was causing all sors of issues - from flickering screen to frozen black screen to crashes. Once I've disabled it and used my second FHD monitor I was finally able to access game settings. 
Hovewer, after "Optimising for your PC" screen and a couple of seconds of loading screen - game crashes. I'm no expert, but I haven't found any obvious errors in proton log, so I've no clue how to overcome this... 
At the time of writing this I own a R9-390, which has weird bugs on linux (just google it) and requires boot parameters to be set for "amdgpu". Maybe my GPU is also the problem. No clue.

title: Forza Horizon 5

notes:
Crashes with `pid N != N-1, skipping destruction (fork without exec?)` on Proton experimental and Proton GE 8.0-14. Manages to make it to menu with Proton 8.0-3 but then gets stuck loading infinitely after pressing "continue".

I also tried a variety of the launch options listed in this thread but they usually led to my GPU driver throwing `NVRM: Xid (PCI:0000:01:00): 69, pid=20042, name=ForzaHorizon5.e, Class Error: ChId 0016, Class 0000c197, Offset 00002490, Data 00000121, ErrorCode 00000004` or the same `skipping destruction` error as above.
title: Forza Horizon 5

notes:
When i try to lanch the game i get "Forza Horzion 5 Compatability Warning" and "Unable to detect graphics driver version."  
If i click "Ignore Warning" the game launches but the whole game window keeps blinking. A few second into launch the system becomes unresponsive and then Plasma Desktop crashes.  
  
Pretty sure i experimented with launch options earlier but didnt take notes since it didn't help.
